title: Passwords Basics
drDigitalIntro: A password is a secret code that only you know — it lets you prove you own an account. Make passwords strong: at least 12 characters, mix uppercase and lowercase letters, numbers, and symbols. Never use your name or birthday. Use different passwords for different accounts — if one is compromised, the others stay safe. Change passwords if you think they've been guessed. Passwords are your first defense against hackers.
drDigitalHint: Strong passwords are long and random.
